title: Membrane AR Wins DCAJ Award at SIGGRAPH E-Tech 2017
summary: David Dunn and colleagues received the Digital Content Expo of Japan Prize in the Emerging Technologies category at SIGGRAPH 2017 for their varifocal AR display system using deformable membrane mirrors.

title: i3D 2017: 2nd Place Paper and 3rd Place Presentation
summary: The GVR group earned 2nd place paper and 3rd place presentation awards at i3D 2017 for "Scene-Adaptive High Dynamic Range Display for Low Latency Display," by Lincoln, Blate, Singh, State, Whitton, Whitted, and Fuchs.

title: Best Paper Award at IEEE VR 2017
summary: The GVR group won best paper at IEEE VR 2017 for "Wide Field of View Varifocal Near-Eye Display using See-Through Deformable Membrane Mirrors" by Dunn, Tippets, Torell, Kellnhofer, Aksit, Didyk, Myszkowski, Luebke, and Fuchs.

title: GVR Varifocal AR Research Featured in RoadToVR
summary: RoadToVR.com covered the UNC/MPI Informatik/NVIDIA research on a varifocal membrane-mirror AR headset achieving a 100° field of view while addressing the vergence-accommodation conflict.

title: Two GVR Papers Accepted to IEEE VR and TVCG 2017
summary: Two GVR papers accepted to IEEE VR 2017: Dunn et al. on varifocal near-eye displays using deformable membrane mirrors, and Chabra et al. on optimizing placement of commodity depth cameras for 3D dynamic scene capture.

title: GVR Paper Accepted to i3D 2017
summary: Lincoln, Blate, Singh, State, Whitton, Whitted, and Fuchs had their paper "Scene-Adaptive High Dynamic Range Display for Low Latency Augmented Reality" accepted to i3D 2017.

title: Long Lasting Impact Paper Awards at ISMAR 2016
summary: Two foundational GVR papers received the Long Lasting Impact Award at ISMAR 2016: Raskar, Welch & Fuchs's "Spatially Augmented Reality" (1998) and Bandyopadhyay, Raskar & Fuchs's "Dynamic Shader Lamps" (2001).

title: Henry Fuchs Keynote at Eurographics 2016
summary: Henry Fuchs, Federico Gil Distinguished Professor of Computer Science at UNC Chapel Hill, delivered the keynote address at Eurographics 2016 in Lisbon, Portugal.

title: Best Paper Award at IEEE VR 2016
summary: Lincoln, Blate, Singh et al. won Best Paper at IEEE VR 2016 for "From Motion to Photons in 80 Microseconds: Towards Minimal Latency for Virtual and Augmented Reality"; UNC also received an Honorable Mention for Rungta, Schissler et al.

title: PD-Insighter Paper Accepted to SIGCHI 2024
summary: Our paper on PD-Insighter, a visual analytics system for monitoring Parkinson's Disease treatment, has been accepted to CHI 2024.

title: Ashley Neall – Best Use of Looking Glass at MIT Reality Hack 2024
summary: Ashley Neall won Best Use of Looking Glass at MIT Reality Hack 2024.

title: Pranav Wagh – Best Healthcare Hack at HackPrinceton 2024
summary: Pranav Wagh won Best Healthcare Hack at HackPrinceton 2024.

title: Praneeth Chakravarthula Rejoins UNC as Tenure-Track Assistant Professor
summary: Praneeth Chakravarthula returns to UNC as a tenure-track Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science.
